---
title: "lims-core"
subtitle: "A Laboratory Information Management System where the audit trail, e-signatures, and chain of custody are properties of the database."
---

lims-core is an open-source LIMS for clinical research: biospecimen and biobank
management for trials first, on a domain-neutral core designed to also serve
analytical labs. It is the third sibling to
[edc-core](https://github.com/tgerke/edc-core) (Electronic Data Capture) and
[ctms-core](https://github.com/tgerke/ctms-core) (Clinical Trial Management);
the three are converging onto one interoperable platform.

![A sample record: storage, aliquot and derivation actions, a DataMatrix label, and versioned, four-eyes-verified results with QC status.](images/04-sample-detail.png){.screenshot fig-alt="lims-core sample detail page showing storage, aliquot and derivation panels, a DataMatrix label, and versioned results with QC status"}

::: {.callout-important}
## Where this is in its life

lims-core has grown well past its first vertical slice: the biobank workflow
(aliquots, shipments, kits, bulk accessioning, freezer map, holds) and a
working analytical layer (specifications, worksheets, QC controls with Westgard
rules, certificates of analysis) both run front-to-back on a production-shaped
compliance core. It is a broad, working build — but **not yet a validated
production system** for running a regulated biobank, and **not a drop-in
replacement for a commercial LIMS**. The honest, code-checked gap analysis is
in the
[completeness review](https://github.com/tgerke/lims-core/blob/main/docs/completeness-review.md)
— read it before planning any deployment.
:::

## Three design commitments

1. **Compliance as schema, not as feature.** The append-only audit trail is
   hash-chained and written by database triggers; results are versioned rows
   that can never be overwritten; signatures are cryptographically bound to
   the exact record version they approve. The application connects as a
   least-privilege role that cannot alter any of this — a bug or a rushed
   hotfix has no path around the controls.

2. **A domain-neutral core.** Samples, tests, results, storage, and custody
   are modeled once. The biobank workflow came first; the analytical-lab layer
   — test specifications, calculated results, worksheets, QC controls with
   Westgard rules, and certificates of analysis — is built on the *same* core
   as a set of modules, not a rewrite.

3. **References, never PHI.** A specimen links to the EDC subject it was
   collected from by reference only. No patient health information enters the
   LIMS; the subject-data boundary between the siblings is architecture, not
   configuration.

## What works today

The founding workflow still holds: one specimen travels from receipt to a
signed, verifiable result — accession → DataMatrix label → freezer storage →
test order → result entry → four-eyes verification → e-signature with password
step-up → a hash-chained audit trail you can verify with one click. Around it,
the [user guide](user-guide/index.qmd) walks the wider surface now in place:

- **Biobank** — [aliquots, derivation, pooling, and holds](user-guide/biobank-operations.qmd);
  [shipments and collection kits](user-guide/shipments-and-kits.qmd) with a
  custody handoff; bulk accessioning, CSV import, and an interactive freezer map.
- **Analytical** — [specifications, worksheets, calculated results, and
  certificates of analysis](user-guide/analytical-testing.qmd), plus
  [QC controls, Westgard rules, and Levey-Jennings trending](user-guide/quality-control.qmd).
- **Inventory & reporting** — a lab-wide [reagent/lot inventory](user-guide/inventory.qmd)
  with an append-only consumption ledger, and [reports](user-guide/reports.qmd)
  for counts, turnaround, and a PHI-free manifest export.

All of it runs front-to-back with OIDC or password auth and grant-based access
scoped to study and site.

## Where to start

| If you are… | Start here |
| --- | --- |
| Trying the app on your machine | [Getting started](getting-started.qmd) — running locally, with seeded demo accounts. |
| Working in the lab (or evaluating the workflow) | [User guide](user-guide/index.qmd) — the app, task by task, with screenshots. |
| Assessing the compliance story | [Compliance](compliance.qmd) — what is enforced, and the test that proves each claim. |
| Sizing the gap to production | [Roadmap and status](roadmap.qmd) — what exists, what is designed, what is not started. |

: {tbl-colwidths="[35,65]"}

lims-core is licensed **AGPL-3.0-only**. Source, decisions, and internal
architecture docs live [on GitHub](https://github.com/tgerke/lims-core).
